data.title: MWG surged 48.72% in after-hours trading, hitting $0.38, following a revenue increase of 87.65% to $26.44 million.
data.description: Shares of Multi Ways Holdings Ltd. (NYSEAMERICAN: MWG) surged by 48.72% in after-hours trading, reaching $0.38 as the company unveiled its first-half 2025 financial results. The Singapore-based firm reported a substantial increase in net revenue to $26.44 million for the six months ending June 30, marking an impressive 87.65% surge from $14.09 million in the prior year's corresponding period. This growth was attributed to robust equipment sales driven by key local infrastructure projects, orders secured in 2024, and a proactive marketing strategy. Multi Ways Holdings also noted a significant spike in net income by 1,025% year-over-year to $0.90 million from $0.08 million. Despite the revenue uptick, the company faced margin pressure during this period. For more detailed insights, refer to the full story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Wego & KTO team up to promote Korea tourism to MENA, aiming to boost travel interest among MENA explorers.
data.description: In Dubai, UAE on December 24, 2025, Wego, the leading travel app and top online travel marketplace in the Middle East and North Africa (MENA), announced a strategic partnership with the Korea Tourism Organization (KTO). This collaboration aims to spark fresh interest in travel among MENA travelers. Wego, known as the number 1 travel app in the region, holds a significant position in the online travel market. The partnership with KTO seeks to showcase new travel opportunities and experiences in South Korea to the MENA audience.

data.title: Tim Cook invests $3 million in Nike shares, acquiring 50,000 shares at $58.97 each, boosting his total to 105,480.
data.description: Tim Cook, the CEO of Apple Inc., has recently increased his investment in Nike Inc. by acquiring 50,000 shares at an average price of $58.97 per share. This transaction, valued at $2.95 million, was disclosed in Securities and Exchange Commission filings. Cook, who has been a member of Nike's board since 2005, now holds a total of 105,480 shares in the company, amounting to $6.04 million based on Tuesday's closing price of $57.34 per share. Despite receiving stock awards worth $179,453 throughout his tenure on Nike's board, this marks Cook's first direct purchase in the open market. The news follows another notable insider purchase by a Nike Director and former Intel Corp executive. More details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Tokyo-based Pacific Bridge Media & Consulting celebrates 15 years offering international media and video production services.
data.description: A communications company located in Tokyo is marking a milestone of 15 years providing support to worldwide organizations through multilingual storytelling, video production, and cross-border communications. The firm, Pacific Bridge Media and Consulting, Inc. (PBMC), has been delivering these services since its establishment in Tokyo.

data.title: WuXi Biologics awarded for ESG disclosure by HERA 2025 in Hong Kong, setting higher standards for sustainability.
data.description: On December 23, 2025, WuXi Biologics (stock symbol: 2269.HK) in Hong Kong was recognized for its dedication to sustainability and transparency. The company was awarded the Outstanding ESG Disclosure at the Hong Kong ESG Reporting Awards (HERA) 2025, showcasing its commitment to environmental, social, and governance practices. This accolade signifies a significant achievement for WuXi Biologics and highlights its efforts in promoting responsible business operations.

data.title: Peter Schiff believes Bitcoin's rally is over as it fails to rise with tech stocks or gold, predicting a bearish future.
data.description: Renowned economist Peter Schiff declared that the rally of Bitcoin, a cryptocurrency, has come to an end, as it has failed to mirror the profits seen in tech stocks or traditional assets such as gold. Schiff expressed doubts about Bitcoin's future growth, stating that if it cannot rise alongside tech stocks or precious metals like gold and silver, then its upward trajectory seems unlikely. According to Schiff, the era of profiting from Bitcoin is over, and if it fails to increase in value, the only direction left is downward. He emphasized that if Bitcoin does not react positively to market movements, it is unlikely to see any significant gains in the future. Schiff's remarks suggest a pessimistic outlook for Bitcoin investors who may face losses if the trend continues.
